key_insight: StatLoRA turns LoRA rank allocation into a hypothesis-testing problem, using p-values from asymptotic score distributions to keep the most statistically significant components under a fixed budget.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a principled way to allocate scarce adaptation rank across layers/modules, potentially improving fine-tuning efficiency and robustness without hand-tuned importance heuristics.
method: Define a test statistic for each LoRA component and estimate its p-value.
Use central-limit theory for stochastic optimizer trajectories to derive asymptotic score distributions, including for AdamW.
Retain or prune components according to a prescribed rank budget based on statistical significance.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on LoRA fine-tuning of DeBERTaV3-base, BART-Large, and Qwen2.5-7B across NLU, NLG, and QA tasks, where it matched or outperformed vanilla LoRA, AdaLoRA, and IGU-LoRA under the same rank budgets.
watch_item: The approach relies on asymptotic approximations and optimizer-trajectory assumptions, so its p-values and pruning decisions may be less reliable in small-data, highly nonstationary, or poorly tuned training regimes.
paper_title: Statistical Inference for Rank Allocation in Low-Rank Adaptation
